extracted_information: The Sourcegraph team migrated from Monaco Editor to CodeMirror due to issues with Monaco's configurability, bundle size, and integration, finding CodeMirror to be more modular, performant, and flexible for their specific needs.

Monaco_Editor.bundle_size: 2.4 MB (40% of Sourcegraph's search page JavaScript download)
Monaco_Editor.default_features: Multi-line editor, code minimap, full IDE features
Monaco_Editor.customization_difficulty: Hard to configure or trim features
Monaco_Editor.css_integration_method: Hard-code hex color codes into JavaScript

CodeMirror.bundle_size_impact: Reduced Sourcegraph's JavaScript download by 43% (from 6MB to 3.4MB)
CodeMirror.default_features: Minimalist and modular; components are added/configured to build desired functionality
CodeMirror.configuration_model: Each instance completely independent, uses local state
CodeMirror.customization_difficulty: Easy to build exact needs via modular components
CodeMirror.css_integration_method: Easily integrates with site-wide CSS classes and variables
CodeMirror.large_file_handling: Intelligently renders only what's on the user's screen

pricing.CodeMirror_funding: MIT license. Social (but no legal) expectation to fund maintenance if used commercially. Sourcegraph donates monthly and sponsors dependencies via thanks.dev.

features.Monaco_capability: Powers VS Code, popular for embedding code editors in browsers.
features.CodeMirror_capability: One of the earliest web-based code editors, adopted by Replit and Chrome's dev tools.

features.Monaco_capability: Gives a lot out of the box, hard to configure or trim down features. Global reference model makes multiple instances with small config differences tricky.
features.CodeMirror_capability: Minimalist and modular; allows building exactly what's needed by adding and configuring components. Each instance is independent.

features.Monaco_capability: Documentation 'not great', making it difficult to determine feature possibility.
features.CodeMirror_capability: Not explicitly stated, but implies ease of use and 'delightful' experience.

features.Monaco_capability: Monaco alone accounted for 2.4 MB (40%) of external dependencies for Sourcegraph's search page, leading to 6MB total JS download.
features.CodeMirror_capability: Reduced JS download to 3.4MB (43% improvement) by replacing Monaco. Doesn't include unnecessary features by default.

features.Monaco_capability: Full multi-line editor by default; difficult to turn into a single-line editor; required custom configuration hacks.
features.CodeMirror_capability: Just worked by default; seamlessly switches between single-line and multi-line.

features.Monaco_capability: Required hard-coding hex color codes into JavaScript, leading to manual synchronization and bugs.
features.CodeMirror_capability: Easily integrates with global CSS classes and variables, allowing automatic theme updates.

features.Monaco_capability: No native support; long-standing open issue since 2017; required hacky workarounds (overlaying elements).
Disabling default keybindings (e.g., Ctrl+F) required messy workarounds.
features.CodeMirror_capability: No need to disable unwanted keybindings; custom keybindings can be added simply.

features.Monaco_capability: Loads and renders entire file, potentially impacting performance for very large files.
features.CodeMirror_capability: Intelligently renders only what's on the user's screen; effortlessly loads documents with millions of lines.

statistics.migration_time_poc: 2 days to replace 90% of existing Monaco functionality with CodeMirror in a proof-of-concept.
statistics.javascript_download_reduction: 43% improvement (from 6MB to 3.4MB) for Sourcegraph's search page JavaScript download by swapping Monaco for CodeMirror.
statistics.monaco_dependency_size: 2.4MB (40% of total JavaScript) for Sourcegraph's search page.

references.description: Business model of open source software
